commands.author: SunnyShu0925
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Signal inbound verbose preview logging to use surrogate-safe truncation and adds focused boundary tests for UTF-16 preview limits.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +52. Total +53 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main formats the Signal inbound body and then calls `body.slice(0, 200)` before verbose logging, which can leave a dangling surrogate when an emoji straddles that code-unit boundary. I did not run the runtime path locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can combine the duplicate imports without changing the PR's runtime behavior or product direction.

target.title: fix(approval): distinguish policy vs non-persistable reason when Allow Always unavailable
target.body:
Fixes #97069.

## What Problem This Solves

When a command includes shell redirection (e.g. `openclaw --version 2>&1`), Allow Always is correctly hidden because one-shot commands can't be safely persisted. But all nine approval prompt renderers (reply, forwarder, agent-facing, Control UI, reaction, embedded handler, Telegram handler, Telegram forwarding, and gateway API) incorrectly blame the effective approval policyŌĆöeven when the effective policy is `ask=on-miss`. Users waste time misdiagnosing their approval policy.

## Why This Change Was Made

The root cause is that `resolveExecApprovalAllowedDecisions()` returns the same `["allow-once", "deny"]` for both `ask=always` (policy) and one-shot persistence (non-persistable command). Downstream renderers can't tell the difference.

Rather than introducing a new typed protocol field (`allowAlwaysUnavailableReason`) ŌĆö which #97081 attempted across 65 files ŌĆö we thread the existing `ask` field (already present in `ExecApprovalRequestPayload`, `ExecApprovalPendingReplyParams`, `PendingApprovalView`, Control UI request state, and the gateway/node host pipeline) through the approval-pending data pipeline so all renderers can branch on it.

The fix is at the renderer layer, not the policy decision layer. Approval policy logic is unchanged.

## Root Cause

`src/infra/exec-approvals.ts:1824` ŌĆö `resolveExecApprovalAllowedDecisions()` collapses both reasons (ask=always policy and one-shot persistence) into a single return value. Nine renderers check `!allowedDecisions.includes("allow-always")` and unconditionally emit the policy message.

Introduced by: gumadeiras `ba735d015809` (generic unavailable copy)
Made visible by: jesse-merhi `c9707ab635b9` (one-shot persistence for shell-redirected commands)
Confidence: clear

## User Impact

Users with `ask=on-miss` (or any non-always policy) who run commands with shell redirection will now see:

> Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).

Instead of the misleading:

> The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable.

Users with `ask=always` see the same policy message as before.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: Approval prompt text blames policy when missing Allow Always is actually caused by one-shot/non-persistable command safety.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node v22.11.0, branch `fix/exec-approval-prompt-text-97069`, base commit `origin/main`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

**Reply renderer** (the function that builds gateway-to-channel approval prompts):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "
import { buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload } from './src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts';

console.log('=== ask=always ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload({
  approvalId: 't1', approvalSlug: 's1',
  ask: 'always', allowedDecisions: ['allow-once', 'deny'],
  command: 'openclaw --version 2>&1', host: 'gateway',
}).text);

console.log('=== ask=on-miss ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload({
  approvalId: 't2', approvalSlug: 's2',
  ask: 'on-miss', allowedDecisions: ['allow-once', 'deny'],
  command: 'openclaw --version 2>&1', host: 'gateway',
}).text);
"
```

**Forwarder renderer** (real `ExecApprovalRequest` objects through the channel-forwarding path):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "
import { buildExecApprovalRequestMessage } from './src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts';

var now = Date.now();
var req = {
  id: 'exec-req-1', createdAtMs: now, expiresAtMs: now + 300000,
  request: { command: 'openclaw --version 2>&1', cwd: '/home/user/project',
    host: 'gateway', agentId: 'main', security: 'allowlist' },
};

console.log('=== ask=always + allow-always excluded ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalRequestMessage(
  { ...req, request: { ...req.request, ask: 'always' } }
));

console.log('=== ask=on-miss + allow-always excluded ===');
console.log(buildExecApprovalRequestMessage(
  { ...req, request: { ...req.request, ask: 'on-miss', unavailableDecisions: ['allow-always'] } }
));
"
```

**Evidence after fix** ŌĆö real production renderer output:

**Reply renderer**:

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "..."

=== ask=always ===
The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable.

=== ask=on-miss (one-shot) ===
Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).
```

**Forwarder** (real `ExecApprovalRequest` objects ŌĆö same shape as the gateway production path):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "..."

=== ask=always + allow-always excluded ===
­¤öÆ Exec approval required
...
Allow Always is unavailable because the effective policy requires approval every time.

=== ask=on-miss + allow-always excluded ===
­¤öÆ Exec approval required
...
Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).
```

**Telegram approval paths** (same renderer called by `buildTelegramExecApprovalPendingPayload` and `approval-handler.runtime.ts`):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "import ... from './src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts'; ..."

=== Telegram: ask=on-miss + allow-always excluded ===
Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persisted (e.g., shell redirection or dynamic content).

=== Telegram: ask=always + allow-always excluded ===
The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable.
```

| `ask` input | Message shown |
|---|---|
| `"always"` | "The effective approval policy requires approval every timeŌĆ”" (unchanged) |
| `"on-miss"` (one-shot) | "Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persistedŌĆ”" (fixed) |
| `"off"` (full-auto with shell redirect) | "Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be persistedŌĆ”" (fixed) |
| absent / null (plugin, legacy) | "The effective approval policy requires approval every timeŌĆ”" (safe default) |

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts \
    src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts \
    src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts \
    src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts \
    extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts
# all passed (with fix in commit 700f47c for approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts ŌĆö see below)
```

**Observed result after fix**: All nine approval prompt renderers now show the appropriate message. Policy ŌåÆ policy text, one-shot ŌåÆ non-persistable text, absent ask ŌåÆ policy (safe default). Production pipeline (gateway + node hosts ŌåÆ ExecToolDetails ŌåÆ embedded handler) threads `ask` through. The node path uses `approvalDecisionAsk` (`maxAsk(hostAsk, nodeAsk)`) ŌĆö keeping the reasoning consistent with `allowedDecisions` computation. Telegram native and forwarding paths pass `ask` through `buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload`. Gateway API error path uses the same `isPolicyAlways` heuristic.

Additional CI guard checks pass locally: `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` (dts emit), `pnpm ui:i18n:check` (all locales clean).

**What was not tested**: Live Gateway with Telegram/Discord channel approval delivery. Telegram terminal proof above exercises the exact same renderer functions called by the Telegram plugin's `buildTelegramExecApprovalPendingPayload` and `approval-handler.runtime.ts`. Mantis automated Telegram Desktop proof will further validate on push via `pull_request_target`.

**Test fix (commit 700f47c)**: The `approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts` test "shows non-persistable reason in reaction prompts" initially used `allowedDecisions: ["allow-once", "deny"]` in the request payload. However, `buildPendingApprovalView()` ŌåÆ `resolveExecApprovalRequestAllowedDecisions()` computes decisions from `ask` + `unavailableDecisions` ŌĆö it does **not** read `allowedDecisions` from the request. The field was silently ignored, `allow-always` was never excluded, and the test couldn't trigger the non-persistable message path. Fixed by changing to `unavailableDecisions: ["allow-always"]`, which correctly feeds into `resolveExecApprovalRequestAllowedDecisions()`. The other two new tests (reply + forwarder) call `buildExecApprovalPendingReplyPayload()` directly with `allowedDecisions` in the payload params, not the request ŌĆö that path uses the field correctly via `resolveAllowedDecisions()`.

## Regression Test Plan

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts \
    src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts \
    src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts \
    src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts \
    src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts \
    extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts
# all pass
```

New test cases added:
- `exec-approval-reply.test.ts`: "shows non-persistable reason when allow-always is excluded for non-policy reasons"
- `exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts`: "shows non-persistable reason when allow-always excluded for non-policy reasons"
- `approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts`: "shows non-persistable reason in reaction prompts when allow-always excluded for one-shot"

## Changes

- `src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts`: +5/ŌłÆ1 ŌĆö branch on `params.ask` in reply renderer
- `src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts`: +5/ŌłÆ1 ŌĆö branch on `request.request.ask` in forwarder
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`: +6/ŌłÆ1 ŌĆö accept `ask` in agent-facing pending message
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-types.ts`: +1 ŌĆö add `ask` to `ExecToolDetails` approval-pending
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.ts`: +3 ŌĆö thread `ask` through `buildExecApprovalPendingToolResult`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts`: +1 ŌĆö pass `hostAsk` to pending tool result
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.ts`: +2 ŌĆö pass `approvalDecisionAsk` to pending result and request registration (not raw `hostAsk` ŌĆö matches the `maxAsk(hostAsk, nodeAsk)` value used to compute `allowedDecisions`)
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`: +3 ŌĆö read and forward `ask` from details
- `src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.ts`: +7/ŌłÆ1 ŌĆö branch on `ask` in reaction manual instruction, with `approvalKind === "exec"` discriminator guard for `PendingApprovalView` union type
- `extensions/telegram/src/exec-approval-forwarding.ts`: +1 ŌĆö pass `params.request.request.ask` to reply payload
- `extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts`: +1 ŌĆö pass `params.view.ask` (exec only) to reply payload
- `src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approval.ts`: +10/ŌłÆ7 ŌĆö distinguish policy vs non-persistable in validateDecision error message
- `ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.ts`: +11/ŌłÆ3 ŌĆö use `allowAlwaysNonPersistable` i18n key for non-policy
- `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts`: +2 ŌĆö new `allowAlwaysNonPersistable` key
- `ui/src/i18n/`: sync-generated fallback keys for all other locales
- 3 test files: +54 new tests covering one-shot render path
target.author: sunnyshu0925

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
